name: Project-native workflow skill integration
description: Add project-owned workflow skills for repo-grounded clarification, issue intake, vertical slicing, prototyping, unfinished-session handoff, and periodic architecture audits; then wire those skills into the current `using-skills` and harness-driven process.

planned: Used `using-skills` first and classified the work as a Level 2 skill-maintenance upgrade because it changes the repo-owned workflow surface across multiple skill files, top-level guidance, plan artifacts, and harness records.
Read `skill-maintenance`, `docs/PLANS.md`, the ExecPlan template, `.agents/README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and current harness conventions before drafting new skills.
Ported upstream workflow ideas manually into repo-native skill folders instead of importing GitHub-only or `CONTEXT.md` assumptions unchanged.
artifacts: Updated `.agents/skills/using-skills/SKILL.md`, `.agents/skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md`, `.agents/README.md`, and `AGENTS.md` to route the new workflow points.
Aligned the `harness/session-handoff.md` template with the new handoff skill by adding a `Suggested Skills` section.
verification: JSON validation passed for `harness/feature_index.json` and `harness/features/feat-074.json` via `python3 -m json.tool`.
Workflow wiring search confirmed the new skill names are referenced from `.agents/README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `using-skills`, and `writing-plans`.
Final `npx gitnexus detect-changes --scope all --repo household-finance-system` returned LOW risk with 8 changed files, 9 changed symbols, and 0 affected processes.

notes: This feature changes only repo-owned workflow docs and skill instructions. It does not change application runtime behavior.
